No blocking findings.

**Acceptance Criteria**
REQ-01/REQ-02 are covered by generic resolution in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:356), with tests for `satisfies`, `per`, `mitigates`, `resolves`, and `enforces` across requirement/decision/risk/invariant in [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:932).
REQ-03 is handled by `dangling_link_findings` in [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:252), surfaced in `validate_silent`, `run_rules`, and scoped validation at [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:208), [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:707), and [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:896).
REQ-04 has unit and real CLI coverage, including [item_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs:582).

**Architecture**
No boundary violations found. Domain item indexing remains pure; command code orchestrates storage/config/domain; validation reporting remains in commands/validation. No new direct persistence path outside storage in production code.

**Functional Paradigm**
The new command-layer validation pass uses simple nested iteration at [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:269). This is acceptable command orchestration and does not introduce meaningful debt.

**Correctness And Invariants**
No atomic-write, event-log, gate-transition, assignee/label-format, or PID-safety regressions found in the changed production files. The resolver uses configured item-kind link namespaces and the existing qualified-id uniqueness invariant from `derive_scope_items`.

**Safety**
`#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains present for the Rust crates. No unsafe code was introduced.

**Testing**
I did not rerun cargo tests, per instruction, because the issue has a passed `cargo-ci` gate. Recorded gate evidence: `./scripts/cargo-ci.sh` passed with fmt ok, clippy ok, and `2498 passed, 0 failed, 12 ignored`.

**Error Handling**
Fallible production paths return `Result`; new errors include context such as the full link label and qualified id. No new production `unwrap()`/`expect()` found outside tests/doc examples.

**CLI Contract**
No new CLI command was added. Existing `validate --json` receives the new `dangling-item-link` finding through `run_rules`, covered by the CLI test at [item_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/item_cli_tests.rs:582).

**Documentation**
New public API surface is documented with examples: `DANGLING_LINK_RULE` at [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:9), `dangling_link_findings` at [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:220), and `resolve_link_label` at [item.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:327).

**Jit Issue Management**
`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed. Both dependencies listed for this issue are marked `done`. No prior review feedback exists in `run_history`.

VERDICT: PASS
